forecast-period.text: For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.

A cold front sweeps across southern SA today. This front will push the inland trough into the far north, exiting the state early Friday. A cooler southwest to southerly flow will gradually moderate in the wake of the front as a strong high pressure system moves in from the west from Friday, establishing in the Bight on Sunday.
* Calls to 1300 number cost around 27.5c inc. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecast or Local Waters Forecast at http://www.bom.gov.au/sa/forecasts/map.shtml for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for these coastal zones.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
Northwesterly 15 to 20 knots tending westerly in the evening. Winds reaching up to 25 knots south of Brighton in the late evening.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the afternoon.
West to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the late morning and afternoon.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Friday for Adelaide Metropolitan Waters
forecast-period.text: Westerly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots south of Brighton during the day. Winds tending southwesterly in the evening.
1.5 to 2 metres.
West to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Cloudy. 80% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots.
1.5 to 2 metres.
West to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Cloudy. 60% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots turning southerly 10 to 15 knots during the evening.
1.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the afternoon.
West to southwesterly below 0.5 metres.
Cloudy.
